title: Bentonite detox face mask
subtitle: A drawing clay mask with bentonite, kaolin, and tea tree for oily and combination skin
excerpt: Bentonite clay carries a negative electrical charge that attracts positively charged impurities, making it an effective drawing agent for congested pores. Kaolin softens the formula for skin types that find pure bentonite too strong. Tea tree essential oil adds a mild antiseptic action at a skin-safe dilution.
sourceNotes: Clay mask formulation from Making Cosmetics guide (makingcosmetics.com, May 2026). Bentonite clay properties from The Clay Cure, Knishinsky (1998). Tea tree EO safety from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 jar dry mask powder (~80 g, approximately 8 applications)
recipe.batchNotes: Store as a dry powder. Mix with water or apple cider vinegar per use. This avoids the need for a preservative and extends shelf life.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the dry powder blend in advance. Mix one to two teaspoons with enough water or apple cider vinegar to form a smooth paste immediately before application.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 18 months as a dry powder in a sealed container. Once mixed with liquid, use immediately: do not store the wet mask.

recipeTools.notes: Do not use a metal bowl or spoon when preparing the wet mask: bentonite reacts with metal ions. Use ceramic, glass, or plastic bowls for the wet application stage.

glossaryTerms.definition: A fine absorbent clay from volcanic ash deposits. Swells on contact with water and carries a negative charge that draws positively charged impurities from the skin. More drawing than kaolin and better suited to oily and combination skin. Do not use a metal bowl when preparing the wet mask: the charge reacts with metal ions.

A temporary increase in breakouts that can follow the first one to three uses of a drawing clay mask. The clay draws congestion from within pores to the surface faster than normal skin turnover, causing a brief flare. Purging resolves within 2 to 4 weeks of continued use. If breakouts persist beyond 4 weeks, discontinue.

content.text: This formula stores as a dry powder and is mixed fresh for each use, which removes the need for a preservative. Pure

can be drying on sensitive or dry skin: kaolin softens the formula. The 60:40 bentonite-to-kaolin ratio works for oily and combination skin; reverse it for normal or sensitive skin. Do not use a metal spoon or metal bowl when mixing the wet mask: bentonite reacts with metal ions.

is expected in the first week or two of using a drawing clay mask. Apply once or twice a week at most. Leave on for 10 minutes: no longer. Remove with a damp cloth before the clay dries completely to avoid over-drying the skin. Follow with moisturiser.

items.prepNote: 60% of formula; the main drawing clay
37.5% of formula; softens the formula for sensitive patches
2% of dry powder weight; add drop by drop and sift through to distribute evenly
0.5% of dry powder weight; optional; balances the tea tree note

content.text: Combine the bentonite clay and kaolin clay in a bowl and stir. Add the essential oil one or two drops at a time, rubbing each addition through the powder to prevent clumping. Sift through a fine-mesh sieve. Transfer to a sealed jar.

To use: place 1 to 2 teaspoons of dry powder into a ceramic or glass bowl. Add enough apple cider vinegar or water to form a smooth, spreadable paste: usually about the same volume of liquid as dry powder. Apply to a clean face avoiding the eye area. Leave for 10 minutes. Rinse thoroughly with warm water and follow with moisturiser.
18 months as a dry powder in a sealed container. Keep dry. Mix only what you will use in a single session and discard any unused wet paste.
